steps.title: Fastest path: connect arc measure to circumference
steps.content: For arc-length questions, relate the arc's angle measure to $360^\circ$ before doing algebra. The arc length is the same fraction of the circumference as the arc's angle measure is of $360^\circ$.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: A 45° arc is $\frac{45}{360} = \frac{1}{8}$ of the full circle, so arc length = $\frac{1}{8} \cdot C$. Set that equal to 5: $$5 = \frac{1}{8} \cdot C$$
